title: Harlech / Llandanwg, Shell Island, Lake Vyrnwy, St. Melangell's

main_route.excerpt: We stopped for tea near a pass before a big slate mine. Llan Bleanow Ffestiniog.

main_route.original_wording: campsite south of Harlech, Llandanwg village. Glan y Gors campsite.
main_route.excerpt: Then onto the campsite south of Harlech, Llandanwg village. Glan y Gors campsite.

main_route.original_wording: wild camping place for the night, on Wales second highest pass, Hellfire Pass, between Lake Bala + Lake Vyrnwy
main_route.excerpt: drove back via Barmouth then up a v. windy, hairy high valley road to a wild camping place for the night, on Wales second highest pass, Hellfire Pass, between Lake Bala + Lake Vyrnwy.
We drove along a v. windy road to Lake Vyrnwy After the dam we stopped for bread, loo paper, the Times + Tea.

main_route.original_wording: a village near St. Melangell's church
main_route.excerpt: roads to a village near St. Melangell's church. We had lunch outdoors at the pub – scampi + Lasagna.
We camped at Llan fechain, quite near Welshpool

stops.excerpt: We cycled against a strong head wind to Shell Island. The previous day the wind had blown me over while I was doing yoga. We cycled around Shell Island – a few shops + lots of campers + sand dunes.
drove back via Barmouth then up a v. windy, hairy high valley road

stops.normalized_name: St. Melangell's Church, Pennant Melangell, Wales
stops.excerpt: After lunch we went on a walk 2½m to St. Melangell's church, an old pilgrimage site. Her shrine was dismantled in the Reformation.

boundary_flags: mixed_end: B_0355_L is marked boundary_mixed=true; the next trip begins on this page or immediately after
summary: Simon and Eve drove from Birmingham to north Wales in August 2021, staying at Glan y Gors campsite in Llandanwg (south of Harlech) for several nights, cycling to Shell Island and exploring the dunes. They then drove via Barmouth, wild-camped at Hellfire Pass between Lake Bala and Lake Vyrnwy, and on Saturday visited Lake Vyrnwy and St. Melangell's Church before a final overnight at Llanfechain near Welshpool.
extraction_notes: No specific calendar dates given; only day names (Thursday, Friday, Saturday) used. Date precision is month only (2021-08 from date_guess). The diarist appears to be Eve. The Shell Island trip (Friday) involved cycling there and back to the van at Llandanwg, but they then drove on to wild-camp at Hellfire Pass the same day after returning to the van, so Barmouth appears as a transit on the drive rather than a separate overnight. St. Melangell's church was visited as a post-lunch walk from the pub in the village (seq 6 main_stop), so it is listed as an excursion from that main_stop rather than a separate main route entry. B_0355_L is marked boundary_mixed=true, indicating the next trip begins on that page.
